what_it_does: It acts as a living accumulation point for candidate laws and principles extracted from multiple project sessions, preserving each source's wording and provenance, grouping apparent duplicates into numbered candidates, surfacing unresolved collisions to a named arbiter, and recording one confirmed law plus open questions about a four-axis coordinate system.
observations: Every fragment is tagged with its source session or channel ID and kept in its original wording before any grouping.
Candidate groupings are explicitly labeled as proposals (CAND-*) and are not silently merged; only the named arbiter can promote a candidate to a law.
One law is already confirmed (LAW-1) and includes a chief-exception refinement added during arbitration.
The file declares an epistemic rule that confident writing does not imply truth and that nothing graduates from candidate to law without direct arbitration.
It proposes four coordinate roots for a definition space, with roots 1 and 2 treated as settled and roots 3 and 4 left open with competing trees.
It distinguishes a second connection four-tuple (inter/intra/intro/retro) from the coordinate axes.
It mixes process laws, structural laws, and meta-method laws in the same registry.
standouts: The governing epistemic note: confidence of writing is not confidence of truth.
LAW-1 CHANNEL-RELAYED-IS-PROPOSAL plus THE CHIEF EXCEPTION, confirmed by direct arbitration.
CAND-9 Heart ≡ Factory convergence, where two sessions independently reached the same fractal-registry/typed-verb-edge structure.
CAND-10/CAND-13 distinction between coordinate axes and connection relations.
The live ROOT-3/4 arbitration presenting Tree A and Tree B side by side without choosing.
conventions: Uses source-channel IDs to preserve provenance on every fragment.
Labels every grouping with a CAND-* or LAW-* identifier and a status.
States rules in uppercase slug form.
Embeds unresolved disputes in the document rather than resolving them offline.
References external artifacts by name without inline definitions.
Repeats the arbiter-gate whenever a candidate might be promoted.
concerns: High information density and session-specific jargon may make confirmed vs provisional claims hard to scan.
The nothing-is-right disclaimer coexists with LOCKED and HIGH confidence labels, which could blur the candidate/law boundary.
Roots 3 and 4 have two competing trees that are not yet arbitrated, leaving the axis model structurally incomplete.
Many cross-references are undefined inside the file, so meaning depends on outside memory.
The candidate grouping step already interprets relationships before the arbiter has ruled.
notes: A dense, provenance-first draft registry that keeps every session's wording and leaves the hard merges open for the named arbiter.
questions: What do the channel IDs map to in terms of session role or time?
What is the exact operational step for promoting a CAND-* to LAW-*?
What are FRD, DNA, the Heart, wildcard, and math-projection in this context?
How is the source equation's scale-phase-lock formulated?
What distinguishes the chief from the lead and from Tim in authority?
How do the coordinate four roots relate to implementation types, registries, and parsers?
What does NO-GREEN-PAINT mean literally as a build practice?
Is the connection four-tuple an implementation relation or a conceptual one?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to accumulate, preserve provenance, reduce duplicates into candidates, and surface collisions to the arbiter; the file does exactly that and also serves as a live worksheet for the unresolved four-axis model.
apparent_themes: Human arbitration as the only promotion gate.
intention_signals: It is trying to turn many parallel session outputs into one shared, arbiter-governed law-set while keeping every source's distinct wording, and to settle a formal multi-axis model for describing code and design definitions.
novelty: It treats project conventions as a legal registry with candidate/law status, provenance tags, and explicit unresolved arbitration, rather than as a flat style guide or decision log.
contribution: A provenance-preserved inventory of multi-source fragments, a candidate-grouping idiom with arbitration hooks, one confirmed law with its refinement, and an explicit unresolved axis-tree decision artifact that a fused system could continue from.
summary_for_embedding: LAW_REGISTRY.md is a provenance-preserved accumulation point for candidate project laws drawn from multiple sessions, grouping possible duplicates into numbered candidates, surfacing unresolved collisions for a named arbiter, and recording one confirmed law plus competing four-axis coordinate models.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to consolidate parallel session outputs into a single arbiter-governed law registry and to work out a shared four-axis definition-space model.
suggested_fields: arbiter_ruling (a structured record of each direct confirmation and its refinement)
source_channel_glossary (mapping channel IDs to session roles and dates)
candidate_evidence_map (which sources converge on each candidate and how)
axis_tree_variant (which competing root tree a statement belongs to)
